name: env-ready
description: Shows a splash page and status-bar indicator while waiting for configured VS Code extensions to activate. Perfect for Codespaces and dev containers where the editor opens before the language tooling is ready.

envReady.waitFor.description: Extension IDs to wait for before showing 'ready'. e.g. ['ms-python.python', 'ms-vscode.live-server']. If empty, the extension does nothing.

envReady.timeoutSeconds.description: Maximum seconds to wait before showing 'ready' anyway, even if some extensions haven't activated.

envReady.loadingText.description: Text shown in the status bar while extensions are activating.

envReady.readyText.description: Text shown when all configured extensions have finished activating.

envReady.showReadyToast.description: Show a one-time notification toast when the environment becomes ready.

envReady.hideAfterSeconds.description: After becoming ready, hide the status-bar item after this many seconds. Set to 0 to keep it visible.

envReady.showSplash.description: Show a fullscreen splash page in the editor area while waiting for extensions to activate. The splash auto-closes when all listed extensions are ready. Set to false to use only the status-bar indicator.

envReady.splashTitle.description: Title shown on the splash page.

envReady.splashMessage.default: Loading the extensions you need to start coding. This usually takes just a few seconds.
envReady.splashMessage.description: Description text shown on the splash page.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.mjs --production
